techniques.evidence_quote: We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.
By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.
leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key
the attacker gained unauthorized access to ... a subset of our AWS S3 buckets
Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.
unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained an AWS access key and an SSH private key that belonged to Datadog's automated provisioning/release (CI/CD) systems.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.

evidence_chunks.quote: We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.
We traced the intrusion to the leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key that were used by our automated provisioning and release systems.

attack_chain.description: Attacker authenticated to AWS using the leaked access key, gaining unauthorized access to Datadog's AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.

evidence_chunks.quote: By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.
By using those particular keys together the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances and a subset of our AWS S3 buckets.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the leaked SSH private key to access three production EC2 instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: leak of an AWS access key and a SSH private key ... the attacker gained unauthorized access to three of our AWS EC2 instances

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed a subset of S3 buckets containing user credentials, service metadata, and third-party integration credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a subset of our AWS S3 buckets. Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.

evidence_chunks.quote: a subset of our AWS S3 buckets. Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.
a subset of our AWS S3 buckets. Those AWS resources included user credentials for the Datadog service, service metadata, and credentials shared with Datadog for third-party integrations.

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed a production database that stores user credentials (bcrypt-hashed passwords) and third-party integration credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials

evidence_chunks.quote: unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials
unauthorized activity associated with a handful of production infrastructure servers, including a database that stores user credentials

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to pivot by reusing stolen customer AWS credentials against customer environments (reported as unsuccessful login attempts).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A user also has reported unsuccessful attempts to use AWS credentials shared with Datadog.

evidence_chunks.quote: A user also has reported unsuccessful attempts to use AWS credentials shared with Datadog.
A user also has reported unsuccessful attempts to use AWS credentials shared with Datadog.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated credentials and metadata from S3 and compromised infrastructure before activity ceased on 2016-07-08 at 06:18 UTC.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Forensics show the attacker activity commenced on 2016-07-07 at 19:44 UTC and ceased on 2016-07-08 at 06:18 UTC.

evidence_chunks.quote: Forensics show the attacker activity commenced on 2016-07-07 at 19:44 UTC and ceased on 2016-07-08 at 06:18 UTC.
Forensics show the attacker activity commenced on 2016-07-07 at 19:44 UTC and ceased on 2016-07-08 at 06:18 UTC.

initial_access_vector: Leaked AWS access key and SSH private key from automated provisioning/release (CI/CD) systems used together to access AWS EC2 and S3.
persistence_mechanism: None reported; attacker session active for ~10.5 hours before detection.
privilege_escalation: None explicitly reported; leaked keys already had access to production EC2 and S3 resources.
